steps.title: Use the trend's sign
steps.content: The data rise from left to right, so the slope must be positive. That eliminates choices A and C.

steps.title: Use the y-intercept
steps.content: The scatterplot is near $y=15$ when $x=0$, so the $y$-intercept should be positive and close to $15$. Choice D — $y = -14.7 + 10x$ has intercept $-14.7$, far below the data.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice B — $y = 14.7 + 10x$, $y=14.7+10x$, has positive slope and a positive intercept near $15$. It matches the trend, so Choice B — $y = 14.7 + 10x$.
